name: Wayfarer Classic
description: Classic trapezoidal acetate sunglasses with G-15 glass lenses. The iconic Wayfarer shape has remained unchanged since 1952.
whyThisFitsMyArchive: The trapezoidal shape suits my face without drawing attention to the fact that I'm wearing sunglasses. G-15 lenses let me see correctly instead of turning everything brown or grey. Black acetate becomes invisible when I wear black.
whyIChooseThis: Over Aviators: The proportions suit me better. Over designer sunglasses: This works without being precious. Over other vintage frames: Wayfarer is refined.
whatItPairsWithInMyCollection: Every outdoor context. Black clothing, cream clothing, everything. These sunglasses don't compete visually.
whatMakesItTimeless: The trapezoidal shape has been correct since 1952. G-15 is permanent technology. Black is permanent.
whatProblemItSolves: Sunglasses that don't announce themselves. Wayfarer disappears.
whatCouldReplaceIt: Persol 649 for Italian alternative. Different Wayfarers in other sizes. But the classic shape is specific.
similarPiecesIWouldProbablyLike: Cartier Santos (same invisibility through proportions). Samba OG (same heritage authenticity).
alternativeUnder3000: Vincent Chase by LensKart (~₹1,500-₹2,000) — clean shapes, no brand cachet
alternativeUnder10000: Carrera Sunglasses (~₹5,000-₹8,000 at LensKart) — sportier but cleaner than Ray-Ban
premiumAlternative: Persol 649 (~₹20,000 on Persol India or LensKart) — Italian acetate, polarized, more refined
buyRecommendation: Buy Polarized — the G-15 polarized version at ₹13,491 on AJIO Luxe is the right choice; non-polarized standard lenses at ₹9,990 are a false economy given India's light conditions. Ray-Ban India official or LensKart are most reliable for authenticity.
